rulings.Case Title: FELL HOLDINGS LLC VS. FELL ST AUTOMOTIVE CLINIC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff Fell Holdings Llc'S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Unseal Record And For Entry Of Judgment Pursuant To Breach Of Stipulation; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Declaration Of M. Ryan Pinkston
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for December 12, 2022 line 1. PLAINTIFF FELL HOLDINGS LLC'S NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION TO UNSEAL RECORD AND FOR ENTRY OF JUDGMENT PURSUANT TO BREACH OF STIPULATION is GRANTED. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: KRISS MIRANDA, PROPERTY AGENT VS. EDITHA BONIFACIO ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Defendant Editha Bonifacio'S Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for December 12, 2022 line 4. NOTICE OF MOTION AND DEFENDANT EDITHA BONIFACIO'S MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS is GRANTED with leave to amend for Plaintiff to address in good faith the issue raised in the moving papers. Plaintiff shall have 5 days from notice of entry of order to file an amended complaint. No opposition filed. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). rulings.Case Title: TENNESSEE STREET HOUSING CORPORATION VS. CULLEN LAFFITY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Set Aside Set Aside Or Modify 11-29-2022 Discovery Order
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for December 12, 2022 line 5. DEFENDANT CULLEN LAFFITY'S NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION TO SET ASIDE SET ASIDE OR MODIFY 11-29-2022 DISCOVERY ORDER is GRANTED based solely on CCP 473(b), excusable neglect due to Defendant's medical condition. The proof of service for the November 28, 2022 hearing is valid and the Court had jurisdiction to proceed. The Court notes that there is no valid proof of service on file for this motion attesting to hand-delivery of the motion by a person claiming to have effectuated (not caused) the service. However, the Court will proceed on the merits given that Plaintiff must have been served and filed an opposition on the merits. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: ALEKSANDR RYVKIN VS. PHILL DEAN NARANJO ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for December 12, 2022 line 6. DEFENDANT PHILL NARANJO'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is OVERRULED, 5 days to answer. Complaint is not based on allegations covered by SFRO 37.9(o). Demurrer is not a proper means to raise factual disputes regarding allegations contained in the complaint as to nuisance. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.
